summary: Iteration 3 — recover-by-email flow tested end-to-end. 28/28 backend pytest pass (incl. 6 new /api/reports/recover tests + updated quiz rate limit to 20/min + new recover 3/min rate-limit test). Frontend Playwright verified /recover page, form submit, results cards, card->/report navigation, empty state, footer recover-link, and 429 toast.
critical_code_review_comments: Recover endpoint projection correctly excludes _id and only returns session_id/score/risk_level/risk_label/paid_at/created_at. Good.
Unpaid-session exclusion is enforced at query level (paid:True) — safe against free-user enumeration.
Endpoint uses slowapi @limiter.limit('3/minute') with real-client-IP key_func (X-Forwarded-For aware) — consistent with other rate-limited endpoints.
Minor: recover response does not include a 'total' count — acceptable given the UI computes count from sessions.length.
Minor: RecoverPage inline click handler on <li> is fine, but consider adding role='button' and keyboard handler for a11y.
seed_data_creation: Per-test seeded paid sessions via POST /api/quiz/submit + /api/checkout/mock with TEST_*@example.com emails; unique UUID per test to avoid collisions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Recover endpoint: POST /api/reports/recover {email} -> {email, sessions[<=5]}. Rate limited 3/min per IP; use distinct X-Forwarded-For headers when testing at volume. Quiz submit limit is now 20/min. test_recover.py + updated test_rate_limits.py are the authoritative regression suite for this feature.
